title: sorbus sitchensis subsp. californica
intro: Sorbus sitchensis subsp. californica, also known as California mountain ash, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family native to the Pacific coast of North America. This deciduous shrub or small tree is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to help lower fever and reduce inflammation, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. The health benefits of Sorbus sitchensis subsp. californica include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, Sorbus sitchensis subsp. californica is often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and salves that can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin irritations and respiratory problems.
